title: How do I promote academic integrity using Canvas Classic Quiz?
plain_text: Canvas Classic Quizzes is an assessment tool that tests students' knowledge of a particular subject matter area. This article will share tips to help promote quiz and exam integrity when creating a Canvas Classic Quiz. Review How do I design an effective online exam that promotes academic integrity? to learn more about promoting exam integrity. Keys to maintain exam integrity: Customize quiz options. When creating a Canvas Classic quiz, there are several options you can choose to help maximize the security of your exams and quizzes. Learn more about what options you can set in a Canvas Classic Quiz. Select 'Shuffle Answers'. This option shuffles the answers for multiple-choice questions. Note: Be careful about using this option if you have questions for which the order of the answers is important. Set a 'Time Limit'. Once the time limit is reached, a student's quiz will automatically submit itself. Customize quiz responses view and feedback: Depending on which options you choose, you can allow students to see the correct answers to a quiz only once (immediately after they complete it), or between two specified dates/times. Hide quiz responses: For maximum security, you can hide students' quiz responses completely by deselecting 'Let Students See Their Quiz Responses'. Hide correct answers or set dates for viewing correct answers: You can hide the correct answers to a quiz in student feedback. Select 'Show one question at a time'. Students will only be able to view one quiz question at a time during the quiz. Fill out the 'Assignment availability' window. Customize quiz questions: Use 'New Question Group': Utilizing Canvas Question Groups allows you to add questions to a select group but randomizes the questions for each student. Limit variables on 'Formula Question' types: When you create a quiz question of type 'Formula Question', you can introduce variables and specify a range of values for each. Resources: What are the test, exam, and quiz options in Canvas? How do I create and grade a quiz in Canvas? How do I set a quiz time limit and availability window in Canvas?
body_html:
Canvas Classic Quizzes is an assessment tool that tests students' knowledge of a particular subject matter area. This article will share tips to help promote quiz and exam integrity when creating a Canvas Classic Quiz. Review How do I design an effective online exam that promotes academic integrity? to learn more about promoting exam integrity.

Keys to maintain exam integrity

Customize quiz options

When creating a Canvas Classic quiz, there are several options you can choose to help maximize the security of your exams and quizzes. Learn more about what options you can set in a Canvas Classic Quiz.
  • Select 'Shuffle Answers'.
  • Set a 'Time Limit'.
  • Customize quiz responses view and feedback.
  • Select 'Show one question at a time'.
  • Fill out the 'Assignment availability' window.

Customize quiz questions

  • Use 'New Question Group'.
  • Limit variables on 'Formula Question' types.

Resources

  • What are the test, exam, and quiz options in Canvas?
  • How do I create and grade a quiz in Canvas?
  • How do I set a quiz time limit and availability window in Canvas?
